refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time for each phase of the load→travel→dump cycle to meet the customer-defined target.
refined_fr.rationale: Decomposing the overall cycle time KPI into sub-functions for each phase allows targeted design parameters and clearer verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for load, travel, and dump phases separately under duty cycle and site conditions; verify each meets allocated target.

candidate_dps: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for load phase efficiency
Optimize vehicle propulsion and navigation systems for travel phase efficiency
Optimize vehicle dumping mechanism and control for dump phase efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for load phase efficiency; Optimize vehicle propulsion and navigation systems for travel phase efficiency; Optimize vehicle dumping mechanism and control for dump phase efficiency

potential_sources: Interdependence between load and travel phases due to vehicle state
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically separable by phase but require interface coordination; coupling is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle subsystems can be optimized independently per phase
Risk of phase transition delays impacting overall cycle time
High safety criticality requires robust control and fail-safe mechanisms
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit interface definitions between phase control DPs to reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and control systems responsible for load, travel, and dump phases of the cycle

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends cycle phase status and performance data to NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and cycle targets from NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends safety alerts to Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for remote monitoring and control

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle module fully contains the physical and control aspects of cycle phases; low diffusion risk.
NGHE Solution oversees but does not directly implement cycle phase controls; higher diffusion risk if assigned here.
Teleoperation involved in control but not primary for cycle time optimization; high diffusion risk.
Safety Services focused on alerts, not cycle time control; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning cycle time optimization DPs to Vehicle module ensures tight containment of physical and control functions, minimizing diffusion and interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with NGHE Solution for tasking and telemetry
Risk of insufficient coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical transitions
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and safety interlocks between Vehicle and Safety Services modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement into load, travel, and dump phases aligns well with selected DPs targeting phase-specific optimizations.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk by assigning all cycle time DPs to Vehicle module
Interface complexity with NGHE Solution and Safety Services noted but manageable
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment scores; recommended to maintain this assignment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry and tasking interfaces with Vehicle module; safety alerts monitored via Safety Services.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of cycle time functions to Vehicle module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Vehicle and Safety Services for safety-critical alerts during cycle transitions.
Consider adding explicit coordination DP/interface for phase transitions to reduce coupling.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet criteria for clarity, containment, and verification feasibility with manageable risks.

revise.why: Refine interface contracts and safety interlocks between Vehicle and Safety Services modules to mitigate identified risks.

assumptions: Vehicle subsystems can be optimized independently per cycle phase
Safety Services will reliably receive and act on SafetyAlert interface messages
open_risks: Phase transition delays impacting overall cycle time
Interface complexity between Vehicle and Safety Services
evidence_needed: Performance data from telemetry during load, travel, and dump phases
Validation of safety alert responsiveness and effectiveness
